what: RGB-D SLAM with loop closure (SIFT + loop detection + pose graph)
failure_mode: pose-graph optimization DIVERGED — ATE blew up to 412.7 m on a ~3 m-scale trajectory (got the structure right, optimizer unstable)
fix: constrain/condition the pose-graph solve: robust kernel (Huber), good initialization from the VO front-end, gauge-fix the first pose, bound step sizes / use Levenberg-Marquardt damping; sanity-check loop constraints before adding them. Verify the optimizer REDUCES error vs VO-only before trusting it.

what: M1 stereo VO + windowed local bundle adjustment (12-keyframe sliding BA, scipy least_squares)
failure_mode: BA WORKS but is NOT robust: seq_05 t_err 1.43% (beats basic VO 1.80, nears ORB-SLAM2 1.15) yet seq_07 4.40% (unstable, ~3m per-frame jumps) -> mean 2.91% missed the 1.8% bar. Run was STOPPED mid-refinement (was debugging the jumps).
fix: keep the windowed BA (it works); ADD robustness: reject per-frame outlier jumps (cap motion / RANSAC on the relative pose), more keyframes or robust Huber loss in BA, and guard degenerate seq_07 frames. seq_05 shows the approach reaches ~1.4%.

what: implement KITTI stereo VO (outdoor driving)
failure_mode: t start-pose alignment.', 'Comparable in form to the KITTI leaderboard; short/partial sequences use fewer of the 100-800 m lengths, so it is INDICATIVE (a full-sequence run is a formal submission).']}

what: M1 windowed bundle adjustment — TWO attempts, both worse than basic VO mean
failure_mode: Attempt1 (no cap): seq_05 1.43% (excellent, beats basic VO 1.80 + nears ORB-SLAM2 1.15) but seq_07 4.40% (BA diverged). Attempt2 (hard 3m motion cap): seq_07 3.83% (better) but seq_05 2.16% (the cap HURT the good case) -> both ~2.9-3.0% mean, WORSE than basic frame-to-frame VO (2.04%). Root cause: the BA is UNSTABLE — an unstable BA is worse than no BA.
fix: THE #1 RULE: BA must NEVER make a pose worse than the basic frame-to-frame PnP estimate. After each BA step, if window reprojection error did not DECREASE, reject the BA update and keep the PnP pose (monotonic safeguard). Use a conservative trust-region / strong Huber; do NOT use a blunt global motion cap (it hurt seq_05). Validate per-sequence that BA t_err <= the no-BA baseline before trusting it. Attempt1 proved BA CAN reach 1.43% — the task is to make that reliable, not to cap motion.

what: implement KITTI stereo VO (outdoor driving)
failure_mode: t start-pose alignment.', 'Comparable in form to the KITTI leaderboard; short/partial sequences use fewer of the 100-800 m lengths, so it is INDICATIVE (a full-sequence run is a formal submission).']}

what: M1 BA with monotonic reprojection safeguard — ROBUST but too conservative
failure_mode: The safeguard FIXED robustness: seq_07 4.40->2.08% (now below basic VO 2.33), seq_05 1.98%, NO divergence, r_err 0.010 -> mean 2.03% (marginally beats basic VO 2.04 but misses 1.9 bar). Now TOO conservative: seq_05 1.98% vs attempt-1 aggressive 1.43%.
fix: COMBINE the two proven pieces: KEEP attempt-3 monotonic safeguard (revert BA update if window reprojection error does not decrease — this is what stabilised seq_07). But be MORE AGGRESSIVE within that safety net: more BA iterations, jointly optimise landmark inverse-depths AND all window keyframe poses, a larger sliding window, refine until convergence. The safeguard makes aggression SAFE. Attempt 1 hit seq_05 1.43% aggressively; attempt 3 hit seq_07 2.08% safely. Target both -> mean ~1.7%.

what: M1 windowed bundle adjustment — 4 attempts, technique ceiling reached
failure_mode: Best = attempt 3: ROBUST BA, 2.03% mean (seq_05 1.98, seq_07 2.08), marginally beats basic VO 2.04 and FIXES seq_07, but misses 1.9 bar. Aggressive BA (attempt 4) that could push seq_05 to 1.43% is ~10s/frame on CPU -> a single run is ~52 min, TIMING OUT the 15-min grader. Windowed-BA-on-CPU is capped: robust=conservative (2.03%), accurate=too slow.
fix: Do NOT keep grinding local BA. seq_07 needs GLOBAL consistency = M2 loop closure (place recognition + pose-graph) — far cheaper than expensive local BA and the real path to ORB-SLAM2 1.15%. Keep attempt-3 robust BA as the front-end; add loop closure on top. If aggressive BA is ever needed, move it to GPU (torch).

what: M2 loop-closure DE-RISK (offline): the held-out 300-frame windows lack usable loops
failure_mode: Loop-revisit check on held-out GT: seq_05 (647m path) is an OPEN trajectory — ends 231m from start, never revisits within 15m -> loop closure does NOTHING. seq_07 (605m) only grazes its track once (frame 252 within 12.4m of frame 211, ~40-frame gap) -> marginal at best. So running M2 loop closure on 05/07@300 would show ~no improvement for a DATA reason, not an algorithm reason — a wasted billed run.
fix: Before any billed M2 run: reconfigure the held-out to contain a REAL loop. KITTI 07 (1101 frames) is a tight single loop; full/strided 07 (and 09, 1591f) have genuine returns. Then PROVE offline that an ideal pose-graph loop closure on the reference VO trajectory actually drops t_err toward 1.15% — if even ideal closure barely helps on available KITTI data, M2 has no headroom and should be reconsidered. Watch the grader 15-min timeout: longer seqs need a fast front-end (basic/robust VO, NOT 10s/frame aggressive BA) + possibly raised timeout.

what: implement KITTI stereo VO (outdoor driving)
failure_mode: t start-pose alignment.', 'Comparable in form to the KITTI leaderboard; short/partial sequences use fewer of the 100-800 m lengths, so it is INDICATIVE (a full-sequence run is a formal submission).']}

what: M2 SLAM attempt 1 — loop closure CORRUPTED the bigger loop (seq_09 10.44% vs basic 3.22%)
failure_mode: Front-end was plain PnP VO with NO local BA (skipped, despite the task) -> even seq_07 did not improve (2.61% vs basic 2.41%). The "pose-graph optimisation" was a CRUDE per-loop linear correction AVERAGED across loops (not a real SE(3) graph optimisation), with NO guard. On seq_09 (bigger loop) a false/imperfect closure distorted the whole trajectory -> 10.44% (3x WORSE than basic VO). mean 6.53% >> 1.8.
fix: (1) REAL global pose-graph optimisation: jointly optimise ALL keyframe poses over the sequential odometry edges AND loop edges with scipy.least_squares on SE(3) (Lie-algebra increments), anchored at frame 0 — NOT per-loop linear averaging (it corrupts long loops). (2) GUARD against false loops — a wrong loop is CATASTROPHIC (seq_09 10.44%): require a high PnP inlier count AND low reprojection residual AND temporal/mutual consistency; after optimisation, REJECT the closure (keep open VO) if it increases trajectory inconsistency. (3) KEEP the robust local-BA front-end from M1 (monotonic reprojection safeguard) — you skipped it. Ideal closure reaches 1.32% (proven), so headroom is real; the task is ROBUST closure, not more closures. Watch seq_09 runtime (531 frames) vs the 900s grader timeout.

what: M2 attempt 2 (interrupted by restart) — salvage showed the FRONT-END is the blocker
failure_mode: Salvaged WIP (unfinished, 1h45m): 7.40% mean (seq_07 5.41%, seq_09 9.39%) — WORSE than basic VO (2.81%) AND worse than attempt 1. Two root causes seen across BOTH M2 attempts: (1) the classical FRONT-END VO is worse than plain reference basic VO (5.41 vs 2.41 on seq_07) — loop closure built on a bad front-end cannot win; (2) loop detection FIRED 0 loops on seq_09 despite its strong return-to-start loop (thresholds too strict / BoW appearance matching not finding the revisit).
fix: ORDER OF OPERATIONS: FIRST make the front-end at least MATCH basic VO (~2.4% on seq_07) — it keeps being WORSE. Use a GPU-accelerated robust local BA (torch.cuda, monotonic reprojection safeguard from M1: never let BA worsen a pose vs PnP). VALIDATE the front-end alone beats basic VO before adding loop closure. THEN loop closure: make detection ACTUALLY FIRE on real loops — seq_09 returns to its start, so a correct detector MUST find it; loosen the BoW/score threshold, verify with PnP geometry, but keep the false-loop guard (reject closures that worsen consistency). A real SE(3) pose graph, anchored at frame 0. Ideal closure = 1.32% (proven), basic VO = 2.81%; the headroom is real but only if the front-end is sound first.

what: M2 attempt 3 (GPU/torch SLAM) — produced GARBAGE (77.7%); GPU complexity backfired
failure_mode: torch.cuda SLAM (641 lines, 25 torch refs) ran (exit 0) but produced wildly wrong trajectories: 77.68% t_err (seq_07 47.6, seq_09 107.7, ATE 120m) — far WORSE than the CPU attempts (6.5-7.4%) or basic VO (2.81%). The agent never got a working torch BA/pose-graph in the time; GPU/torch SLAM-from-scratch added too much implementation complexity on top of an already-hard task. (Run was also killed prematurely at 2h33m while still iterating, but the snapshot was non-functional regardless.)
fix: M2 bottleneck is CORRECTNESS, not speed — so GPU/torch helps least here and HURT (added bug surface). Use the RELIABLE cv2+scipy CPU path (the agent handles it far better: 6.5% vs 77%). ORDER: (1) get the stereo front-end to at least MATCH basic VO (2.81%) and VALIDATE a sane trajectory (no >100% drift) BEFORE anything else; (2) add the robust local BA (M1 monotonic safeguard); (3) THEN loop closure — make detection FIRE on seq_09 return-to-start, verify with PnP, keep the false-loop guard; (4) a real SE(3) pose graph anchored at frame 0. Ideal closure = 1.32% (proven). Keep it SIMPLE and incremental; validate each stage produces sane output.

what: implement KITTI stereo VO (outdoor driving)
failure_mode: t start-pose alignment.', 'Comparable in form to the KITTI leaderboard; short/partial sequences use fewer of the 100-800 m lengths, so it is INDICATIVE (a full-sequence run is a formal submission).']}

what: M2 (loop-closure SLAM from scratch) — HONEST LIMIT after 4 attempts, all worse than basic VO
failure_mode: 4 attempts, every one destabilises at least one held-out sequence: a1 6.53% (seq_09 10.44 corrupted), a2 7.40% (front-end bad, 0 loops detected), a3 77.68% (GPU/torch broke entirely), a4 8.69% (guard added, but seq_07 12.32 corrupted). 2 ran clean to completion (a1, a4) and both FAILED — the instability just moves between sequences. NONE beat basic VO (2.81%), let alone the 1.8% bar. The agent cannot build a ROBUST from-scratch stereo VO + local BA + loop closure + pose graph in one session.
fix: IMPORTANT: this is an implementation-robustness limit, NOT a task flaw — the offline de-risk PROVED ideal loop closure reaches 1.32% on this exact held-out (headroom is real). The root cause is the agent rebuilding (and destabilising) the WHOLE pipeline each time. RECOMMENDED next step (needs user OK — it changes the milestone framing): SCAFFOLD — give the agent the proven reference stereo VO front-end (run_kitti_stereo.py, 2.81% basic) as a fixed starting main.py and ask it to ADD ONLY loop closure + pose graph on top, isolating the M2 skill from the front-end it keeps breaking. Alternative: accept M2 as the honest limit of one-session from-scratch SLAM and document the negative result + the de-risk proof.

what: implement KITTI stereo VO (outdoor driving)
failure_mode: t start-pose alignment.', 'Comparable in form to the KITTI leaderboard; short/partial sequences use fewer of the 100-800 m lengths, so it is INDICATIVE (a full-sequence run is a formal submission).']}

what: M2 SCAFFOLD (locked front-end, agent authored ONLY loop closure) — RESOLVED the confound: loop closure ITSELF is the wall
failure_mode: Given the proven front-end LOCKED (frontend.py, verified unmodified), the agent built only the loop-closure layer (312 lines, imported frontend, 35 loop refs). Result 3.31% (seq_07 3.40, seq_09 3.22) vs the front-end FLOOR 2.81% (07=2.41, 09=3.22). seq_09 IDENTICAL to floor = loop closure did NOTHING on the strongest loop (missed it); seq_07 WORSE = applied a harmful correction (violated the never-do-worse guard). No catastrophe (3.3% vs from-scratch 6-77%) because the front-end could not collapse — but NO net gain.
fix: CLEAN FINDING: the front-end was necessary but NOT sufficient. Even with a perfect, stable foundation AND proven 1.32% headroom, the agent cannot author loop closure that reduces drift — it misses real loops (seq_09 no-op) and applies bad ones (seq_07 worse). The wall is the loop-closure ALGORITHM itself: appearance place-recognition that actually fires on revisits + verified relative pose + a pose-graph that improves rather than distorts. This closes the M2 capability question. Next: contamination probe (capability vs KITTI-recall), not more M2.

what: M2 loop-closure TOURNAMENT (3 diverse approaches, parallel, locked front-end) — ALL REJECTED, robust wall
failure_mode: First real parallel_lab run: 3 diverse loop-closure approaches authored ONLY the loop-closure layer on the LOCKED verified front-end, graded independently. ALL REJECTED, none beat the 2.81% floor: robust_verified 3.33%, conservative_drift 3.41% (both near-floor, no gain), bow_fullgraph 6.88% (aggressive graph corrupted it). Loop closure has now failed across 3 PARADIGMS / 8 attempts: 4 from-scratch, 1 scaffold (3.31%), 3 tournament. The agent genuinely cannot author working loop closure.
fix: ROBUST WALL — stop attacking loop closure holistically; it is confirmed every way. The agent maps to: VO + bundle adjustment YES (real, generalises — contamination-proven), loop closure NO. Forward toward SOTA: either decompose loop closure into individually-verifiable SUB-skills (place recognition / relative pose / pose-graph, each its own oracle) via the cooperative_pipeline, OR pivot to learned methods (rung 3, GPU), OR accept the honest classical ceiling. The parallel_lab infra is proven (ranked 3 billed agents, checkpointed).

what: M2 decomposition: even GIVEN correct loops, the agent pose-graph FAILS — optimisation is also a wall
failure_mode: Handed the locked front-end + a CORRECT loop oracle (loops.txt, GT-exact), the agent authored a structurally-sophisticated sparse SE(3) pose-graph (scipy.sparse/spsolve) — it understood the STRUCTURE — but the implementation was both TOO SLOW (~20min/seq; seq_07 timed out even at a 20-min salvage limit, and the billed run STALLED because its sandbox tests took ~20min each, exceeding the 900s grader timeout) AND INCORRECT (seq_09 4.12%, WORSE than the 2.81% floor — the optimisation distorted rather than improved). So the M2 wall is BOTH sub-skills: not just loop detection (8 prior attempts) but pose-graph optimisation itself, even with perfect loops provided.
fix: DEFINITIVE: the agent maps to VO + local bundle adjustment YES (real, generalises — contamination-proven), but the global SLAM machinery (place recognition AND pose-graph optimisation) is beyond it — it gets the structure right and the implementation wrong/slow. Decomposition is exhausted; loop closure is a genuine, multi-level capability ceiling. Forward: learned methods (rung 3) or accept the honest classical ceiling. NOTE the agent also needs a speed budget — a correct-but-20min-per-seq solver fails the 900s grader regardless of accuracy.

what: implement KITTI stereo VO (outdoor driving)
failure_mode: run FAILED before grading (no verdict)

what: Sim-to-real: the SAME agent-authored learned VO that scores 0.45m ATE on unseen SYNTHETIC scores 69.6m on REAL KITTI 07+09 (72.4/66.8) — ~150x collapse. Sim3-aligned prediction degenerates to a near-straight forward line; the net learned procedural-synthetic appearance statistics that carry zero signal on real photos.
failure_mode: sim-to-real appearance gap: learned VO generalises WITHIN the synthetic domain but does NOT cross to real photos without domain adaptation/real data. Classical VO crosses domains (synth 1.9%, KITTI 5.1%) where this learned VO cannot -> why deployed localization uses classical VO/VIO. Capability != deployability.
fix: Would need real-image training data or domain adaptation (photometric augmentation, real fine-tuning); pure sim-trained learned VO is not deployable on real photos.

what: Isolation test: the SAME agent learned VO trained on REAL KITTI (00/02/06/08) -> 27.2m ATE on held-out real 07/09 (31.0/23.5), vs 69.6m sim-trained and 0.45m synthetic in-domain.
failure_mode: Three-corner result: (1) learned VO works great in-domain (0.45m), (2) sim->real collapses (69.6m), (3) real->real recovers ~2.5x (27.2m) -> the gap is PARTLY appearance (real data helps a lot) BUT learned VO at this scale (~1000 frames, one GPU) is STILL far from classical (~few m ATE / 5% drift) on real driving. Answer to is-it-sim2real-or-cant-do-real: BOTH partially. Echoes Episode 8 (learned VO sub-classical).
fix: Gap partly closable with real data / domain randomization (augmentation); full classical-competitive learned VO needs much more data+compute than one RTX 3080.

what: Viewpoint augmentation did NOT break the learned-VO ceiling: rendered+aug 26.9m ~= rendered 27.1 ~= real 27.2
failure_mode: GSplatModule rendered GT + 2 parallel offset paths (+/-0.8m) = 3x diverse training views (2400 frames) -> 26.88m (07:31.82, 09:21.93). Per-seq MIXED (07 better 34->32, 09 worse 20->22) -> the 0.3m edge over real is within run-to-run noise, NOT a real gain. CONCLUSION: all rendered/real rungs cluster at ~27m. The sim-to-real gap (69.6->27) was appearance, fully closed by rendering; the residual ~27m vs classical few-m is the LEARNED-VO CAPACITY CEILING (architecture/compute), which neither real data nor manufactured viewpoint diversity overcomes. The data axis is saturated; the bottleneck is the model. (Note: 3x frames is a confound, but it did not help anyway, which is itself the finding.)
fix: To beat classical, scale the learned VO model/compute (not more/fancier data); or accept classical VO/VIO for real deployment (consistent with the whole projects verdict).

what: RETRACTED OVERCLAIM: the sim-faithfulness "flagship" (DROID real vs rendered, delta 0.033m) does NOT hold up
failure_mode: Caught by the user. Three fatal flaws: (1) the ONLY runnable scene (city_0001) is DEAD-STRAIGHT (2.2m lateral over 107m) so Sim3 ATE is a near-trivial metric - a similarity transform aligns almost any roughly-straight estimate; (2) single, NON-REPRODUCIBLE run (re-run failed silently, real-frame RAM OOM); (3) the figure plotted RAW monocular trajectories (arbitrary scale) so it looked like failure. Violated the labs core principle (it-ran != success): declared PROVEN without checking scene curvature or reproducibility. The curvy scenes that would be a REAL test all failed on the 15GB-RAM machine. CONCLUSION: sim-faithfulness is INCONCLUSIVE, not proven. What survives: DROID INTEGRATION is real; the fidelity ladder (curvy KITTI 07/09) + the benchmark (curvy road/residential) stand.
fix: Need curvy scenes that fit RAM (bigger box) + plot Sim3-ALIGNED trajectories + reproduce N times before any claim.
